macro_summary: Thursday's session was dominated by a sharp semiconductor selloff (SOXX -5.6%) as Meta's AI cloud push added fresh pressure to hyperscaler-peak fears, while a massive June payrolls miss (57k vs. 129k expected) sent gold surging 2.8% and the dollar lower. The oil de-escalation narrative continued—WTI held near $69 after further advances in US-Iran talks—while a dovish payrolls print dented near-term Fed tightening expectations. The risk-off move was concentrated in semis and AI infrastructure names; the broader S&P 500 closed flat, with defensive rotation evident.
commodities_note: WTI edged up 1.9% to $68.97 but remains down 26% over the past month, reflecting the unwinding Hormuz premium as exports recover. Gold jumped 2.75% to $4,194.70 on the payrolls miss and lower real yields. The 10Y Treasury yield ticked up 0.22% to 4.48% amid the mixed session, supported by Warsh's hawkish refusal to hint at rate changes.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on extension above their 50-day averages, with no NIM relief despite the oil-price normalization. The cluster's forward P/Es are uniformly low, but the MAS S$NEER and US10Y threshold (4.20%) keep the dual headwind intact. No bank offers a near-term entry.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — awaiting NIM relief catalyst (US10Y <4.20% or MAS easing).
singapore.key_development: OCBC cut oil price forecasts (per InvestorsHub, Jul 2), reflecting the unwinding Hormuz premium; this is a modest tailwind for energy-exposed corporate loans but does not overcome the NIM squeeze.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster suffered another brutal session with SOXX -5.6%, led by memory and photonics names. Hard-blocked tickers (MU, AMD, SK Hynix) dominate, while only TSM and NVDA offer technical watch entries amid the rout. The cluster remains structurally bullish but tactically extended — the SOXX benchmark is now 9.4% below its 50-day SMA.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — the semis selloff is a reset, not a reversal.
semiconductors.key_development: Meta's reported AI cloud entry (per Yahoo Finance, Jul 1) intensified hyperscaler oversupply fears, hitting the entire cluster; mid-cap names like LITE and CRWV are at the sharp end of the contagion.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is mixed: AMZN and GOOG hold neutral, while MSFT is momentum-blocked and PLTR is surging but also blocked. The Microsoft Azure OpenAI credit circularity risk keeps MSFT volatile; GOOG's TPU advantage and AMZN's AI capex are intact but not delivering earnings traction yet. No clean entry across the group.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk and binary events cap near-term conviction.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's 5-day +10.7% surge on Kyndryl cloud expansion triggered a momentum block, while AMZN's Prime Day results were solid (per BofA, Jul 3).

interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment mirrors LITE; COHR is the optical interconnects basket peer read-through.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Greensboro fab timeline accelerates and COHR pulls back 15% from current levels.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology (MRVL)
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO, benefiting from the same hyperscaler shift away from merchant GPUs.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO reclaims its 200-day SMA and MRVL's next earnings (Jul 30) confirms AI ASIC revenue above $1B.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS)
interconnected.reason: Third HBM supplier after SK Hynix and Micron — Samsung's HBM3E qualification status is the key read-through on memory supply diversity.
interconnected.entry_note: Reassess after Samsung's next earnings (July 30) if HBM3E yields cross 80% and SK Hynix corrects 10% from here.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks (ANET)
interconnected.reason: AI networking demand from NVDA Spectrum-X and 800G optical rollouts — direct beneficiary of NVDA's data-center buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA's networking revenue exceeds 15% of total in Q2 and ANET pulls back to its 50-day SMA.

active_risks: US-Iran de-escalation: Positive talks in Doha, Hormuz oil exports recovering; Goldman sees deal by end-July. Oil risk premium nearly priced out.
US-China tech cold war: Pentagon blacklist aims at China's AI; MATCH Act threat lingers for ASML; China InP export controls continue to disrupt AXTI.
EU trade regulations: Steel and e-commerce rules tighten against China, adding friction to European semis (IFX, AIXA, SOI).
Fed policy uncertainty: June payrolls miss (57k) dents rate hike bets, but Warsh remains hawkish; 10Y yield at 4.48% still elevated.
geopolitical.market_impact: The semis selloff (SOXX -5.6%) is the primary expression of AI capex anxiety, while defensive rotation into AMZN/GOOG shows BigTech resilience. Singapore banks remain trapped by US rates. Gold's surge reflects a dovish payrolls repricing, but not yet a full risk-off shift.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: June payrolls miss (57k vs 129k expected) released Thursday morning, triggering a sharp gold rally and dollar dip; Warsh's ECB panel speech offered no rate hints. US-Iran Doha talks advanced, with Goldman calling a deal by end-July.

base.description: US-Iran deal finalized, WTI stabilizes near $65, Fed holds rates steady in July. Semis continue to grind lower as AI capex fears linger, but structural demand intact. Singapore banks remain capped by US10Y above 4.20%.

optimistic.description: Cease-fire holds, oil drops below $65, easing inflation pressures and allowing the Fed to signal a pause. AI capex concerns fade as MU earnings and NVDA guidance reaffirm demand — semis rally, with SOXX reclaiming its 50-day, and TSM/ASML break out.

pessimistic.description: Iran talks collapse, Hormuz disruption resumes, WTI spikes to $90. Fed forced to hike rates. Semis plunge further (SOXX -15%), and Singapore banks sell off on NIM compression and risk-off flows.

wildcard.description: Trump imposes 232 tariffs on imported pharmaceuticals, triggering a broad market selloff; a flight to safety pushes gold above $4,400 and hits all cyclicals. BigTech underperforms as trade war fears mount.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions with a defensive tilt; no new entries until the SOXX finds a floor above the May lows or the oil de-escalation triggers a durable dovish Fed pivot.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive with selective entries (TSM BUY, NVDA/ASML WATCH)
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 6 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, while the semis selloff creates valuation opportunities but no clear floor yet. The oil de-escalation narrative reduces macro risk, but earnings binary risk (TSM, ASML, etc.) caps conviction.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
